title: Site Topology Change Detection
dataSources: `index=catalyst sourcetype=cisco:dnac:site:topology`. from the `site_topology` modular input in the Cisco Catalyst Add-on (Splunkbase 7538) polling `GET /dna/intent/api/v1/topology/site-topology`. Default poll interval: 3600s (1h). Volume: ~1 event per site per poll. RBAC: NETWORK-ADMIN-ROLE. Key fields: `siteId` (string), `siteName` (string), `siteType` (string), `parentSiteName` (string). CIM data model: Compute_Inventory.
spl: index=catalyst sourcetype="cisco:dnac:site:topology" | stats earliest(_time) as first_seen latest(_time) as last_seen values(siteType) as types by siteId, siteName, parentSiteName | where first_seen > relative_time(now(), "-7d") | eval change_type="New site added" | sort -first_seen
description: Detects changes to the Catalyst Center site hierarchy — new sites, modified sites, or removed sites — that may require configuration updates.
value: Site hierarchy changes affect device assignment, policy application, and reporting groupings. Detecting them ensures downstream configurations stay aligned.
implementation: Enable the `site_topology` input in the Cisco Catalyst TA pointing to `index=catalyst`. The TA polls site hierarchy data from the Catalyst Center Intent API every 60 minutes. Key fields: `siteId`, `siteType` (area, building, floor), `parentSiteName`, `siteName`. Pair with a longer baseline window if you need to diff deletes.
visualization: Table (siteName, parentSiteName, first_seen, change_type, types), alert when new `siteId` row appears, optional comparison dashboard against CMDB site list.

references.title: Catalyst Center Site Topology API — Cisco DevNet

knownFalsePositives:
**Legitimate site hierarchy expansion for new office or building.** Adding new areas, buildings, and floors to the site hierarchy is normal operational activity. Distinguish by correlating with `index=catalyst sourcetype="cisco:dnac:audit:logs"` for site creation events by authorized administrators during business hours. Suppress by requiring topology changes to be from unauthorized users or outside approved change windows before alerting.

**Site hierarchy reorganization as part of SD-Access fabric deployment.** Deploying SD-Access fabric requires site hierarchy changes (creating fabric sites, assigning buildings). These generate topology change events as part of the deployment workflow. Distinguish by correlating with fabric provisioning audit entries. Suppress by allowing topology changes during fabric deployment windows.

**API automation tool creating or modifying sites programmatically.** Infrastructure-as-code tools or scripts may create or modify sites via the Catalyst Center API. These are authorized changes by service accounts. Distinguish by checking whether the `username` in the audit entry corresponds to a known automation service account. Suppress by filtering service account topology changes from the anomaly detection.

**Catalyst Center upgrade migrating site hierarchy structure.** Platform upgrades may restructure the site hierarchy (adding hierarchy levels, migrating legacy structures). Distinguish by checking whether topology changes cluster around a Catalyst Center upgrade event. Suppress by establishing a new topology baseline after the upgrade.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Cisco Catalyst Add-on for Splunk (Splunkbase 7538) with the **site** modular input enabled to `index=catalyst`, `sourcetype=cisco:dnac:site`.
• At least 7 days of historical site data for baseline comparison.
• UC-5.13.51 operational (site hierarchy inventory is a prerequisite for change detection).

Step 1 — Configure data collection
Site topology change detection compares the current site hierarchy poll against previous polls to identify additions, deletions, and modifications. No additional data collection configuration is needed beyond UC-5.13.51.

The TA polls the site hierarchy API at the configured interval (default 3600 seconds). Changes are detected by comparing the current poll's site list against the previous poll.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert

```spl
index=catalyst sourcetype="cisco:dnac:site"
| stats latest(_time) as last_seen earliest(_time) as first_seen latest(name) as current_name latest(siteHierarchy) as current_path by siteId, siteType
| eval days_since_first=round((now()-first_seen)/86400,1)
| eval is_new=if(days_since_first < 7, "New Site", "Existing")
| eval days_since_last=round((now()-last_seen)/86400,1)
| eval is_missing=if(days_since_last > 2, "Possibly Removed", "Active")
| where is_new="New Site" OR is_missing="Possibly Removed"
| table siteId, siteType, current_name, current_path, is_new, is_missing, first_seen, last_seen
| sort is_new, -first_seen
```

Understanding this SPL:
• **`stats latest(_time) ... earliest(_time) ... by siteId`**: Tracks when each site was first and last seen. New sites have a recent `first_seen`; removed sites have a stale `last_seen`.
• **`is_new=if(days_since_first < 7, ...)`**: Flags sites first seen within the last 7 days as new additions.
• **`is_missing=if(days_since_last > 2, ...)`**: Flags sites not seen in the last 2 days as potentially removed. Uses 2 days (not 1) to account for occasional TA poll failures.

Step 3 — Validate
• **Test event:** Create a new floor in Catalyst Center > Design > Network Hierarchy. Wait for the next site poll cycle (up to 1 hour). Verify the new floor appears in the Splunk results with `is_new="New Site"`.
• **Remove test:** Delete the test floor. After 2 days, verify it appears with `is_missing="Possibly Removed"`.
• **Vendor UI parity:** cross-reference the Splunk results with the corresponding **Catalyst Center > Assurance** page for the same time window to confirm counts and values match.
Step 4 — Operationalize
• **Dashboard:** Two-panel layout — "New Sites (Last 7 Days)" table and "Possibly Removed Sites" table. Add a timechart of total site count trend over 30 days.
• **Alert:** Trigger on new area or building additions (not floor additions, which are more routine). Route to the network planning team for capacity review.
• **Change management:** Correlate site topology changes with `index=catalyst sourcetype="cisco:dnac:audit:logs" auditResourceType="SITE"` to identify who made the change.

Step 5 — Troubleshoot
• **False "Possibly Removed" alerts during TA outage:** If the TA was offline for >2 days, all sites will appear as "Possibly Removed" until the next successful poll. Check TA health before investigating topology changes.
• **New sites not detected:** Increase the search time range to cover the site poll interval (default 1 hour). If using a 24-hour time range and the site was added less than 1 hour ago, it may not appear yet.
• **No site topology events arriving:** verify the `site_topology` input is enabled and check `index=_internal` for API error or endpoint timeout messages from the TA.
• **Missing or empty values in site hierarchy:** newly created sites may have empty parent references until the hierarchy is fully configured in Catalyst Center.
grandmaExplanation: We catch when new buildings or floors are added to the network map, which means configurations and policies may need updating.
